scope.summary: Send files uploaded to the cloud encrypted file store through the hosted zca-rust session, so cloud-only attachment bubbles represent real Zalo sends and survive refresh.
goals: Expose a backend send-file API for hosted cloud accounts
Validate device auth plus user/account/file ownership before decrypting any file blob
Decrypt file blobs only in backend memory and upload via zca-rust AttachmentSource::Data
Persist encrypted outgoing rich file metadata so desktop refresh keeps the sent attachment bubble
Wire the desktop cloud upload flow to call hosted send-file after encrypted blob upload
nonGoals: Background retry queue for failed Zalo attachment uploads
Production media transcoding or thumbnail generation

acceptance.description: A cloud device uploads an encrypted file blob, asks the backend to send it to a hosted Zalo thread, receives a non-secret response, and can refresh the desktop while the outgoing file message remains hydrated from cloud storage.

verification.manual: Attach backend/frontend gates plus API or unit evidence proving authorization, encrypted storage reuse, no plaintext logs/storage, and refresh-safe rich file metadata.
